last_item: Band-2.5 (resource & config safety): self-healing config — a config change that stops Postgres auto-rolls-back to last-known-good; PG never left down.
last_result: done (iter 26) — built the self-healing config primitive in internal/pg/safeconfig.go: snapshotAutoConf captures <data_directory>/postgresql.auto.conf BEFORE a restart-requiring change (fails closed if unreadable), restartWithRollback restarts PG and on a non-zero systemctl restart exit (authoritative for the synchronous postgresql unit) restores the snapshot via tee as the postgres user (keeps owner+0600; works while PG is down, unlike ALTER SYSTEM RESET) and restarts again so PG is never left down, returning CodeSafety ('rolled back; Postgres running') or CodeInternal ('Postgres is down', with manual-fix hint) if rollback restart also fails. Routed EnsureArchiving's restart path through it (snapshot before its ALTER SYSTEM loop) so it's load-bearing now, and fixed ensureBackupConfigured to stop re-wrapping the typed error as generic CodeInternal (was burying the SPA's CodeSafety/CodeInternal branch). Tests: acceptance (bad setting → exact-content rollback → PG up → CodeSafety), rollback-restart-also-fails → CodeInternal down, no-rollback-on-success, snapshot-fails-closed, and the snapshot-before-write ordering invariant in EnsureArchiving. Go gate green (gofmt/vet/test/build, sandbox-disabled per snap); web untouched. Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): addressed all 3 findings. Remaining band-2.5: host-sized tuning at provision, connection-saturation alert — both must funnel any restart through this primitive.
notes: BAND 1.5 + BAND 2 COMPLETE. IN BAND 2.5 (resource & config safety). DONE iter 26: SELF-HEALING config primitive (internal/pg/safeconfig.go): snapshotAutoConf captures <data_directory>/postgresql.auto.conf BEFORE a restart-requiring change (fails closed if unreadable); restartWithRollback restarts PG and on non-zero `systemctl restart` exit (authoritative for the synchronous postgresql unit) restores the snapshot via `tee` as the postgres user (keeps owner+0600; works while PG is down, unlike ALTER SYSTEM RESET) + restarts again so PG is never left down -> CodeSafety ('rolled back; PG running') or CodeInternal ('PG down'+hint) if rollback restart also fails. Routed EnsureArchiving's restart through it (snapshot before its ALTER SYSTEM loop) + fixed ensureBackupConfigured to stop re-wrapping the typed error as CodeInternal. ANY future config-apply/override flow MUST funnel its restart through restartWithRollback (snapshot before write). DONE iter 25: disk-headroom-low early-warning rule (WARNING 80% host.disk_percent, For 5m/Cooldown 1h) below critical disk-almost-full (90%). KEY: adding a disk/conn alert is usually RULE-ONLY -- Snapshot already has disk_used/total_bytes + connections + max_connections, alert/metrics.go metricValue computes host.disk_percent / pg.connections_percent, and background.go's seedDefaultAlertRules inserts only missing IDs (idempotent) so a new DefaultRules() entry auto-seeds on upgrade. Test pattern: extend TestDefaultRules want-list + a focused engine test; background_test asserts len(DefaultRules()) dynamically. REMAINING band-2.5 open items: (c) host-sized tuning at provision (shared_buffers/work_mem/effective_cache_size/max_connections from detected RAM/CPU per DEFAULTS.md; pure sizing fn, test against sample RAM values, no PG needed). (d) connection-saturation alert as active conns approach max_connections -- LIKELY ALREADY SATISFIED: connections-near-max ALREADY EXISTS in DefaultRules() (WARNING pg.connections_percent >= 85, For 2m). AUDIT it; if sufficient, delete the backlog item with a one-line reason, else add an early/critical tier. --- Alert plumbing: Collector.SampleOnce -> Engine.Evaluate -> dispatchAlertEvents (background.go); metricValue (alert/metrics.go) reads Snapshot fields; go is a snap -> run go/psql/pg_ctl sandbox-disabled; empty $TMPDIR in some shells -> use /tmp. --- HISTORICAL: band 1.5 (backups proven-restorable, S3 nudge, restore-test verify+deep, scheduled jobs) and band 2 (web silent-failure audit, idempotent provisioning, query-box auto-LIMIT+statement_timeout enforced) all COMPLETE. See git log.
north_star: An indie hacker can stand up and run a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI, on best defaults, with safe optional overrides.
